<!-- Generated by the agentic-foundation-survey workflow (2026-06-08): a 6-cluster fan-out over legacy/Agentic + synthesis. A survey/handoff doc, not an ADR. -->

> **Owner decisions since this report (2026-06-08):** §7 **OQ1** (where red-before-green is
> enforced) is **resolved** by [ADR-0020](../decisions/0020-red-green-enforcement-on-the-owned-loop.md)
> — spine-side phase machine + per-phase write-scoping + executor-owned RED/GREEN observation.
> §7 **OQ6** (DBOS now or later) is **decided: DBOS later** — the phase machine / `runLoop` step
> recorder lands on plain Postgres (the event store) first, not DBOS-durable steps (ADR-0019,
> reaffirmed in ADR-0020). The remaining OQs (2–5) are left for the fresh build session.

# Foundation Report: Extracting the Picks-and-Shovels from `legacy/Agentic` for storytree V2

## 1. TL;DR

**Take (in priority order):** (1) the **orchestration step-machine** — `step.rs` / `sequence.rs` / `run_loop` — the deterministic spine that owns control flow while the model only judges inside a leaf; (2) the **red-green / prove-it discipline** — red-before-green as forensic evidence, separation of authoring authority, commit-SHA-pinned fail-closed verdicts, "a halted iteration is never a pass," operator-attested as a signed event; (3) the **signer-identity chain** (flag → env → git config → typed error, fail-closed at every tier); (4) the **single narrow Store interface** with the docs-vs-append-only split and trait-parity tests (re-grounded on Postgres). **Leave:** the entire `claude`-CLI-subprocess runtime (`runtime.rs` / `compose_argv` / stream-json plumbing), sandboxing (owner-scoped OUT), the surrealkv backend, `agentic-work` epics/phases, the 3,800-line `session-orchestrator` git/worktree/merge-ceremony apparatus, the brownfield story-mapper, and git pre-commit hooks as the enforcement point. The story-modeling cluster is **already ported** into `packages/core/src/schema.ts` — it's a knowledge/guardrail item, not code.

## 2. The picks (ranked)

| Part | Verdict | Why | Effort | Lands in |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Step machine: `run_step` / `run_step_validated` / `run_sequence` / `run_loop` + the "halted-is-never-a-pass" and budget/early-exit guards | **adapt** | The load-bearing spine/leaf split (ADR-0005/0011). Deterministic code owns routing; LLM judges only inside a step. The hard-won false-green guard ports verbatim. | medium | `packages/orchestrator` |
| Owned leaf turn loop (rebuilt on Messages API) | **adapt** | The primary pick. Agentic's leaf is a subprocess wrapper (dead); the *turn discipline* (drive tools → feed results → loop to `end_turn`) is what survives, on the raw SDK. | large | `packages/agent` (replaces `pi-adapter`) |
| Red-green / verify / prove-it-gate discipline | **adapt** | The honesty machinery. Red-before-green as evidence, authority separation, fail-closed-on-dirty-tree, commit-SHA pins the tree. Must be re-pinned per V2 tier. | large | `packages/core` (types) + `packages/orchestrator` (verifier) |
| Signer-identity chain (`agentic-signer`) | **take** | ~80 lines, ports almost verbatim. Answers ADR-0007 open-q §1 (who signs). Fail-closed, no silent fallback. | small | `packages/core` (or `packages/proof`) |
| Store interface + docs/append split + trait-parity tests | **adapt** | One narrow `Store` every consumer writes through; backend swaps without touching call sites. Re-ground on Postgres/JSONB + zod-at-write. | medium | `packages/core` + thin `packages/store` |
| Typed event vocabulary (`claude_event.rs`) | **adapt** | Loud-on-malformed typed parsing of model output. Becomes a typed union over the SDK's native content blocks, not an NDJSON re-parse. | small | `packages/core` |
| Agent-definition triple (contract/inputs/process YAML) | **adapt** | The *shape* (owns/does_not_touch/authority + required_reading + workflow) is the right encoding; strict-schema discipline. Drop the `contract.yml` filename and accreted prose. | small | `agents/` dir + loader in `packages/core` |
| Story/work modeling (`agentic-story`, schemas) | **leave** | Already absorbed into `packages/core/src/schema.ts` (3-tier discriminated union) + `loader.ts`. Knowledge/guardrail only. | (done) | — |
| Orchestration disciplines (orient-before-acting, thin-by-design, blind-reverify, surface-don't-decide) | **adapt** | Port as prompt/return-contract patterns + a code-review invariant, NOT as new TS services. | medium | `packages/orchestrator` + prompts |

## 3. The owned agent loop (concrete anatomy for the TS rebuild)

The Agentic "loop" is **two layers**, and only one survives. Layer A is `agentic-runtime/src/runtime.rs` — it spawns the `claude` binary as a subprocess (`compose_argv`: `claude -p <prompt> --output-format stream-json --input-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions`), pipes a JSON envelope to stdin, reads NDJSON back. **This is dead on arrival** (ADR-0011's cautionary tale). Layer B — `step.rs` / `sequence.rs` — is the genuinely valuable orchestration spine and ports conceptually.

**Turn structure (the new leaf, `packages/agent`).** Rebuild on `@anthropic-ai/sdk`:

- `runTurn(ctx)`: `messages.create({ model, system, messages, tools })` → for each `tool_use` content block, dispatch via a `ToolExecutor` interface (the sandbox impl is borrowed *elsewhere* and is OUT of this cluster — the loop only sees the interface) → append a `tool_result` block → loop until `stop_reason === 'end_turn'`. Keep the model call behind a thin internal seam (ADR-0011 §3) so it's swappable and mockable.
- **Do not** carry over the iteration-counting heuristic in `runtime.rs` (it increments on a `tool_result` that follows a `tool_use` — a stream-shape hack). On the Messages API an "iteration" is a clean request/response turn.

**Model-API call & event vocabulary.** `claude_event.rs` (the typed, loud-on-malformed parser of System/Assistant/User/Result events and ToolUse/ToolResult/Text blocks) becomes a **typed union over the SDK's native content-block types** in `packages/core` — not a re-parse of someone else's NDJSON. Keep the discipline: schema drift surfaces as a typed error, never a silent count-of-zero.

**`runStep` (port `step.rs`).** Return a discriminated union `StepResult = { ok: true, output, structuredOutput, sessionId } | { ok: false, error: 'NoTerminalResult' | ... }` — **fail-closed, not throw-on-missing**. A step that never reaches a terminal result returns the error variant; the sequence halts on it rather than advancing on an empty-but-OK value. `run_step_validated` → compile a JSON schema once with **ajv (Draft 2020-12)** (or zod), validate `structuredOutput`, bounded retry. Typed verdicts drive control flow, never substring matching.

**Sequence & loop (port `sequence.rs` / `run_loop` into `packages/orchestrator`).** `run_sequence` = ordered steps, splice each result into the next prompt via a caller-supplied closure (an explicit, testable context hand-off seam — not implicit conversation state), halt on first failure. `run_loop` = repeat until a structured verdict passes OR an iteration budget is exhausted (exact-iteration-count observable). **Realize these as DBOS-durable steps** (ADR-0005/0011): each step is a workflow step; budget/early-exit live in durable state. Port the **`loop_halt_no_false_pass`** guard verbatim — the pass-arm is gated on `halted_at.is_none()` so a passing verdict on a non-terminal step of a halted iteration can never be mistaken for success. Cite: `tests/loop_until_pass.rs`, `tests/loop_halt_no_false_pass.rs`, `tests/loop_budget_exhaustion.rs`.

**Test seam.** `Runtime` was a trait with `MockRuntime` / `ScriptedRuntime` doubles living in `src` (reused by tests, not re-hand-rolled). Preserve this: the entire loop must be exercisable with zero live model calls behind the model seam.

**Subagent spawn/brief.** Keep the discipline, not a service: read-only fan-out is **parallel-by-default**, write-bearing spawns are **serial** (different hazards). Brief with the **shape of the answer** you need (a typed verdict: `findings` / `assumption_violations` / `summary`), not a checklist of where to look — represent these as typed structured-return contracts in `packages/core`, so callers parse rather than read prose. **No agent-spawns-agent**: the orchestrator is the sole fan-out point.

**The agent-spec triple.** `agents/<cat>/<name>/{contract,inputs,process}.yml` — `contract.yml` = scope/owns/does_not_touch/authority + outcome/success_criteria; `inputs.yml` = required_reading + context globs + tools + commands; `process.yml` = session_start + ordered workflow + guidance.rules. **Carry the SHAPE, retire the filename** (ADR-0002 frees the word "contract" for the leaf tier) and **leave the accreted prose** (`session-orchestrator/contract.yml` is 200KB+ of retired-ADR archaeology — `risks` flag it explicitly). Load + validate strictly (zod) at startup; port `agentic-agent-defs`' `Registry` sketch (`load_from_dir`/`get`/`validate_all`) to a TS `AgentRegistry` — but note `agentic-agent-defs` was **README-only, never implemented**, so treat it as a design note. `required_reading` should evolve into ADR-0011's pull-based, just-in-time context assembly, not a static read-everything list.

## 4. The TDD discipline, evolved to V2

Agentic proves work with: red-before-green as forensic evidence, separation of authoring authority, commit-signed fail-closed verdicts, and a UAT path that is the only road to `healthy`. V2 keeps the *disciplines* but re-pins them across **three tiers** where **the proof mode IS the tier boundary**.

**What changes — concretely:**

1. **Red-before-green moves to the CONTRACT tier only** (ADR-0007). A test must be observed failing for the right reason (compile-red = missing symbol; runtime-red = assertion/panic) before implementation, and the red diagnostic reads the contract back to a human. **The central porting risk:** in Agentic this honesty came from *process isolation* — separate crates (`agentic-test-builder` can't write `src/`, `build-rust` can't write `tests/`) plus hard agent authority walls. **V2 owns one streamed loop (ADR-0011), so those walls are gone** — the red-before-green honesty must now be enforced by **spine-orchestrated phasing over one agent**. If that phasing is weak, the agent trivially writes test+impl together and forges green. Build the phase gate first, not last.

2. **The prove-it-gate generalizes to three tiers:**
   - **contract** = one isolated automated test (stubs allowed *only* at the declared cross-story interface seam; a stub *within* the organism is a structural defect).
   - **capability** = ≥1 integration test + its contracts green against **real in-story collaborators**.
   - **story** = ≥1 integrated UAT end-to-end.
   `agentic-verify`'s per-story verifier becomes a **per-tier verifier with proof-mode-aware collaborator rules**. Re-express as a `Verifier` pure over `(unitSpec, treeState, env) → Verdict`, never mutating status, with a pluggable `TestExecutor` / `UatExecutor` interface (Agentic's `UatExecutor` trait + `StubExecutor` + `RecordingExecutor` port cleanly) so the collaborator-realness rule swaps per tier.

3. **`operator-attested` becomes a first-class signed event** reaching `healthy`, *distinct in the audit trail* from a UAT sign — for surfaces with no honest UAT or isolatable test (e.g. the orchestrator's own routing discipline). Agentic's 4-tier signer-resolution + `uat_signings`/`manual_signings` rows are the seed. **An agent can never self-attest** — keep this hard. The `backfill_manual_signing` eight-guard path in `agentic-store` is the ancestor, but **one of its guards is admittedly weak** (guard 3 / "YAML-flip-in-history" only checks HEAD has a parent — `risks` say "do not faithfully port a known-weak guard; redesign properly").

4. **The forensic floor carries verbatim:** every verdict signed by `git rev-parse HEAD` **and** a clean `git status --porcelain` — "a Pass without a commit is forgeable." Dirty tree ⇒ typed refusal, zero rows written. The commit SHA pins *which* tree was attested; the signer pins *who*. This holds even though evidence moves **from git-committed JSONL into the shared Postgres store** (ADR-0017) — preserve append-only / atomic / partial-write-tolerant, but the writer is `store.append`, not `fs.rename`. **Risk:** moving rows to Postgres weakens "the commit signs the verdict" unless the commit-hash + clean-tree check remain the row's trust anchor.

5. **Earned-status invariant:** `healthy` must remain **non-authorable** — reachable only through a signed proof event. V2's `Status` enum lists `healthy` as a plain value, so enforcement must live in the **gate/loader**, not be hoped for in the zod enum.

6. **Reward-hacking guardrails** port as lint/spine rules: no shared assertion helpers (a single route to green-N-tests), no `#[ignore]`/`assert!(true)`/trailing-panic equivalents, **assert DEPTH (terminal effect) not SHAPE (signature/return-Some)**, fixtures must mirror production failure modes. **Do NOT port** the cargo-test stdout parsing, rustc-ICE handling, syn-based parse checks, or Windows heredoc workarounds — all environment-specific.

7. **Ancestor-health gate splits by altitude:** cross-story edges are **declared-interface-only** (ADR-0002/0010), within-story capability edges are **code-derived** — so "you can't prove a unit standing on an unproven ancestor" applies at two grains. Port the cycle-detection IDEA (three-colour DFS, ~30 lines), not the v1 flat-graph assumption. Watch for **"ghost-healthy" drift** (YAML says healthy, store has no/stale signing row).

## 5. Explicitly left behind

- **Sandboxing / tool execution** — owner-scoped OUT entirely. The loop sees only a `ToolExecutor` *interface*; the impl is borrowed from open source in a separate effort (ADR-0012).
- **`runtime.rs` subprocess machinery** — `compose_argv`/`compose_command`, the stdin JSON envelope, env-scrub (`CLAUDECODE`), stream-json flags, `--dangerously-skip-permissions`, `ClaudeCodeRuntime`. Claude-CLI-specific plumbing with zero value on the Messages API; copying its structure re-imports the opacity ADR-0011 rejects.
- **The iteration-counting heuristic** — a stream-shape hack; on the native API an iteration is a clean turn.
- **surrealkv backend + its concurrency dance** — byte-key namespacing (`U//A//M/` prefixes, `0x00`/`0xFF` bounds), the owned-tokio-runtime + async/std double-mutex append-serialization. Postgres gives transactions and sequences natively. Leave 100%.
- **`agentic-events` as code** — unbuilt README. Salvage *only* the `EventEnvelope` shape (timestamp/source/correlation_id) + "events are data, correlation IDs first-class." The append-only events table **is** the bus; do not resurrect a Rust event-bus.
- **`agentic-work` (epics + phases + feedback-triggers)** — V2 routes via the story DAG + prove-it-gate; this duplicates and conflicts.
- **`session-orchestrator` process.yml apparatus** — ~3,800 lines of git/branch/worktree/chip/claims/merge-ceremony rules. Take the named disciplines, leave the apparatus (it's the exact 1,100-line-accretion the cluster warns against).
- **`agentic-story-mapper`** — brownfield decomposer with fixture-fitted heuristics (the 1.6-tests-per-story magic number). Separate, later, optional capability — not foundation.
- **git pre-commit hooks as the enforcement point** — agents can `--no-verify`; hooks aren't in fresh clones. Enforce the prove-it-gate **orchestrator-side on the owned loop** where it can't be skipped; treat any git hook as secondary convenience.
- **The clap CLI surface (~30 subcommands)** — keep only the **exit-code contract (0/1/2)** and dispatch-only discipline; the rest belongs to other clusters.
- **`schemaless serde_json::Value` store stance** — explicitly *reversed* for storytree: zod-validated-at-write. The keyed-vs-append "first write fixes the kind" runtime convention becomes a **hard schema-level distinction** (separate tables/DDL).

## 6. Build order for the fresh session

Dependency-ordered; each step is small and shippable. The story schema already exists in `packages/core/src/schema.ts`, so we start at the type/event layer and build outward to the loop.

1. **Typed event/content-block union + `Verdict`/`ProofMode` types** (`packages/core`). A typed union over the SDK's content blocks (text/tool_use/tool_result), `Verdict { outcome, commitSha, runId, timestamp, results[], traceRef }`, `ProofMode = contract | capability | story | operator-attested`, and the evidence/signing row shapes — all zod, validated at write. (Ports `claude_event.rs` + the verdict types.)

2. **Signer chain** (`packages/core` or `packages/proof`). `resolveSigner({ flag }) → Signer | SignerError`, tiers flag → `STORYTREE_SIGNER` env → `git config user.email` → typed error, trimmed-length->0 at every tier, no fallback default. ~80 lines + table-driven tests. (Ports `agentic-signer`.)

3. **`Store` interface + in-memory impl + trait-parity tests** (`packages/core` interface; `packages/store` Postgres impl). `upsertDoc` / `appendEvent` / `getDoc` / `queryDocs` over `pg` + JSONB, zod-at-write, Postgres `BIGSERIAL`/sequence for append ordering. Pin the five behavioral contracts (upsert-replaces, append-preserves-order, typed-absence = `null` not error, empty-query-not-error, shareable) as tests run against **both** the Map-backed double and Postgres. (Ports the `Store` trait + parity suite; drops surrealkv.)

4. **`runTurn` leaf on the Messages API** (`packages/agent`). `messages.create({ tools })` → dispatch `tool_use` via the `ToolExecutor` *interface* (stub impl for now) → append `tool_result` → loop to `end_turn`. Model call behind a thin seam with a `MockModel` double. Zero sandbox. (Ports the surviving half of the runtime.)

5. **`runStep` + `runStepValidated`** (`packages/orchestrator`, or `packages/agent`). Discriminated-union `StepResult` (fail-closed `NoTerminalResult`), ajv/zod-validated structured output with bounded retry. (Ports `step.rs`.)

6. **`runSequence` + `runLoop` as DBOS-durable steps** (`packages/orchestrator`). Splice-composition closure, halt-on-first-failure, repeat-until-verdict-or-budget, and the **`halted-is-never-a-pass`** guard ported verbatim with its own pinned test. (Ports `sequence.rs` / `run_loop`.)

7. **Tier-aware `Verifier` + `proveItGate`** (`packages/orchestrator`). Pure `(unitSpec, treeState, env) → Verdict`, pluggable `TestExecutor`/`UatExecutor`, fail-closed on dirty tree, commit-SHA-pinned, writes signing rows via the `Store`. `healthy` reachable only through a signed event. (Ports `agentic-verify` + `agentic-uat` promotion, re-tiered.)

8. **`workStory(storyId)` thin spine + `AgentRegistry`** (`packages/orchestrator` + `agents/` loader). read unit → assemble context (pull-based) → drive the leaf → gate on recorded evidence (never a model call) → record verdict. Under a few hundred lines; **no retry/DAG/budget subsystem** until a real run earns it (port the "thin by design / earned" note as a literal comment + ADR cross-ref). Load + strictly validate the agent-spec triple. (Ports the `agentic-orchestrator` brain sketch + `agentic-agent-defs` registry sketch.)

## 7. Open questions for the owner (ADR-0018 workflow)

1. **Where does red-before-green phasing get enforced now that the authority walls are gone?** V2 owns one streamed loop — the spine must phase "author red test → confirm red → implement → confirm green" over a single agent. Is this a hard spine state machine, a structured-output gate between phases, or both? This is the central honesty risk; it needs an explicit ADR.
2. **Signer persistence + identity for `operator-attested`** (ADR-0007 open-q §1). The 4-tier resolver answers *who*; does the owner want operator-attested signatures to require a human-in-the-loop confirmation distinct from the git-config identity, or is `git config user.email` sufficient attestation?
3. **Projection model in the store:** keep projection-on-read (Agentic's pure query folds over rows) or add an events→current-state projector? Affects whether `current-state` docs are derived or materialized.
4. **`packages/agent` vs `pi-adapter`:** ADR-0011 names `packages/agent` as provisional, replacing `pi-adapter`. Confirm the rename so the fresh session lands the leaf in the right package (current tree still has `packages/pi-adapter`).
5. **Brownfield "mapped" state in scope?** (open-q §2). The mapped state + its no-red-evidence invariant is a small audit function if brownfield onboarding is a V2 goal — but it's explicitly out of the "less is more" foundation floor. Confirm defer.
6. **DBOS commitment:** the spine being "DBOS-durable steps" is assumed throughout (ADR-0005/0011) but MEMORY notes ADR-0019 defers DBOS to "library" tier. Confirm whether step 6's `runLoop` durability lands on DBOS now or on a plainer Postgres-backed step recorder first.

---
**Key legacy paths cited:** `legacy/Agentic/crates/agentic-runtime/src/{runtime,step,sequence,claude_event,run_recorder}.rs`; `.../agentic-runtime/tests/{loop_until_pass,loop_halt_no_false_pass,loop_budget_exhaustion}.rs`; `legacy/Agentic/crates/agentic-store/src/{lib,surrealstore,session_claims}.rs`; `legacy/Agentic/crates/agentic-signer/src/lib.rs`; `legacy/Agentic/crates/agentic-verify/README.md`; `legacy/Agentic/crates/agentic-uat/src/lib.rs`; `legacy/Agentic/agents/{build/build-rust,test/test-uat,test/test-builder,orchestration/session-orchestrator}/{contract,inputs,process}.yml`; `legacy/Agentic/schemas/story.schema.json`; `legacy/Agentic/docs/decisions/0014-gate-signing-walk-ancestry.md`. **Already-ported targets:** `packages/core/src/{schema,loader}.ts`.
